Name: Cloud Shadow
Description: Simulates large-scale cloud shadows projected onto the world using scrolling textures. Typically aligned with the directional light to create dynamic, moving shadow patterns that mimic passing clouds.

Fields.Name: Enable Cloud Shadow
Fields.Description: Enables or disables cloud shadow projection. When disabled, no cloud-based light occlusion is applied.

Fields.Name: Texture 0: ID
Fields.Description: Identifier of the primary cloud shadow texture used to modulate light intensity across the scene.

Fields.Name: Texture 0: Shadow Scale
Fields.Description: Intensity multiplier for the shadow contribution of this texture. Higher values produce darker cloud shadows.

Fields.Name: Texture 0: UV Offset
Fields.Description: Offset applied to the texture coordinates, allowing manual positioning of the cloud pattern.

Fields.Name: Texture 0: UV Speed
Fields.Description: Scrolling speed of the cloud texture in UV space, simulating cloud movement over time.

Fields.ID: Cloud Shadow Texture0: UV Tilling Size:[m]
Fields.Name: Texture 0: UV Tiling Size
Fields.Description: World-space size (in meters) covered by one repetition of the texture. Larger values result in broader, slower-varying cloud patterns.

Fields.Name: Texture 1: ID
Fields.Description: Identifier of a secondary cloud shadow texture used to add variation or layered detail to the cloud pattern.

Fields.Name: Texture 1: Shadow Scale
Fields.Description: Intensity multiplier for the secondary cloud shadow layer, typically used to break up repetition and add depth.

Fields.Name: Texture 1: UV Offset
Fields.Description: Offset applied to the secondary texture coordinates for independent positioning relative to the primary layer.

Fields.Name: Texture 1: UV Speed
Fields.Description: Scrolling speed of the secondary cloud texture, often set differently from the primary layer to create parallax-like motion.

Fields.ID: Cloud Shadow Texture1: UV Tilling Size:[m]
Fields.Name: Texture 1: UV Tiling Size
Fields.Description: World-space tiling size (in meters) for the secondary texture, typically differing from the primary layer to avoid visible repetition.
